notes: THE_WORKSHOP — Frasier's technical board. Tech threads. Hidden technical unlocks. Skeleton content for sprint 1.

threads.subject: where do you put a heavy thing at the end of the night

op.body:
When the heavy gear gets used hard during the week, it needs a rest stop at the end. I've been running a quiet line for that since '92. Dial 5550108. The rest is the rest. If you've been working the heavy gear and you don't know the dial, you should.

  — F.

replies.body: Confirmed. The rest is the rest.

threads.subject: the cathedral AC · what failed and what to swap

op.body:
Window unit gave up on Wednesday at 11 AM. Compressor's seized. Replacement is a window-mounted 18000 BTU; the bracket's the same; the wiring's old enough that the new unit's pigtail is gonna want an adapter. If anyone's done this swap on a cathedral installation, the wiring run was the surprise — what did you do for the conduit?

  — F.

replies.body: We did the same swap on the boathouse in '93. The conduit was the surprise then too. The trick is fishing a new run alongside the old without pulling the old; the old's the structural ground for the original.
Don't run AC off the same panel as the lighting. We learned that one in '88.
